text: For God so loved the world, that he gave his only begotten Son, that whosoever believeth in him should not perish, but have everlasting life.
The LORD is my shepherd; I shall not want.
I can do all things through Christ which strengtheneth me.
Trust in the LORD with all thine heart; and lean not unto thine own understanding.
Fear thou not; for I am with thee: be not dismayed; for I am thy God.
Come unto me, all ye that labour and are heavy laden, and I will give you rest.
For I know the thoughts that I think toward you, saith the LORD, thoughts of peace, and not of evil, to give you an expected end.
And be ye kind one to another, tenderhearted, forgiving one another, even as God for Christ's sake hath forgiven you.
Peace I leave with you, my peace I give unto you: not as the world giveth, give I unto you.
Blessed are the peacemakers: for they shall be called the children of God.
Be careful for nothing; but in every thing by prayer and supplication with thanksgiving let your requests be made known unto God.
And the peace of God, which passeth all understanding, shall keep your hearts and minds through Christ Jesus.
Depart from evil, and do good; seek peace, and pursue it.
Thou wilt keep him in perfect peace, whose mind is stayed on thee: because he trusteth in thee.
And let the peace of God rule in your hearts, to the which also ye are called in one body.
The LORD will give strength unto his people; the LORD will bless his people with peace.
And he said unto them, Why are ye troubled? and why do thoughts arise in your hearts?
Charity suffereth long, and is kind; charity envieth not; charity vaunteth not itself, is not puffed up.
This is my commandment, That ye love one another, as I have loved you.
Love worketh no ill to his neighbour: therefore love is the fulfilling of the law.
We love him, because he first loved us.
Nor height, nor depth, nor any other creature, shall be able to separate us from the love of God, which is in Christ Jesus our Lord.
And above all things have fervent charity among yourselves: for charity shall cover the multitude of sins.
God is love; and he that dwelleth in love dwelleth in God, and God in him.
Thou shalt love thy neighbour as thyself. There is none other commandment greater than these.
Greater love hath no man than this, that a man lay down his life for his friends.
Give, and it shall be given unto you; good measure, pressed down, and shaken together, and running over, shall men give into your bosom.
But my God shall supply all your need according to his riches in glory by Christ Jesus.
The LORD thy God shall bless thee in all that thou doest.
But seek ye first the kingdom of God, and his righteousness; and all these things shall be added unto you.
Commit thy way unto the LORD; trust also in him; and he shall bring it to pass.
Honour the LORD with thy substance, and with the firstfruits of all thine increase.
He becometh poor that dealeth with a slack hand: but the hand of the diligent maketh rich.
The LORD is my rock, and my fortress, and my deliverer; my God, my strength, in whom I will trust.
This book of the law shall not depart out of thy mouth; but thou shalt meditate therein day and night: for then thou shalt make thy way prosperous.
Now the God of hope fill you with all joy and peace in believing, that ye may abound in hope, through the power of the Holy Ghost.
And hope maketh not ashamed; because the love of God is shed abroad in our hearts by the Holy Ghost which is given unto us.
Happy is he that hath the God of Jacob for his help, whose hope is in the LORD his God.
Rest in the LORD, and wait patiently for him.
Hope deferred maketh the heart sick: but when the desire cometh, it is a tree of life.
But they that wait upon the LORD shall renew their strength; they shall mount up with wings as eagles; they shall run, and not be weary.
Rejoicing in hope; patient in tribulation; continuing instant in prayer.
The LORD is good unto them that wait for him, to the soul that seeketh him.
Thou art my hiding place and my shield: I hope in thy word.
Now faith is the substance of things hoped for, the evidence of things not seen.
If ye have faith as a grain of mustard seed, ye shall say unto this mountain, Remove hence to yonder place; and it shall remove; and nothing shall be impossible unto you.
The just shall live by faith.
But without faith it is impossible to please him.
If thou canst believe, all things are possible to him that believeth.
For we walk by faith, not by sight.
And he said unto him, Arise, go thy way: thy faith hath made thee whole.
For as the body without the spirit is dead, so faith without works is dead also.
Be not afraid, only believe.
For he that cometh to God must believe that he is, and that he is a rewarder of them that diligently seek him.
The LORD is my strength and my shield; my heart trusted in him, and I am helped: therefore my heart greatly rejoiceth.
Be strong and of a good courage; be not afraid, neither be thou dismayed: for the LORD thy God is with thee whithersoever thou goest.
My grace is sufficient for thee: for my strength is made perfect in weakness.
My flesh and my heart faileth: but God is the strength of my heart, and my portion for ever.
Finally, my brethren, be strong in the Lord, and in the power of his might.
He giveth power to the faint; and to them that have no might he increaseth strength.
For God hath not given us the spirit of fear; but of power, and of love, and of a sound mind.
The LORD is my light and my salvation; whom shall I fear? the LORD is the strength of my life; of whom shall I be afraid?
In every thing give thanks: for this is the will of God in Christ Jesus concerning you.
O give thanks unto the LORD; for he is good: for his mercy endureth for ever.
It is a good thing to give thanks unto the LORD, and to sing praises unto thy name, O most High.
Serve the LORD with gladness: come before his presence with singing.
I will praise thee; for I am fearfully and wonderfully made: marvellous are thy works; and that my soul knoweth right well.
Thanks be unto God for his unspeakable gift.
The LORD hath done great things for us; whereof we are glad.
Enter into his gates with thanksgiving, and into his courts with praise: be thankful unto him, and bless his name.
Bless the LORD, O my soul, and forget not all his benefits.
Rooted and built up in him, and stablished in the faith, abounding therein with thanksgiving.
If we confess our sins, he is faithful and just to forgive us our sins, and to cleanse us from all unrighteousness.
Blessed is he whose transgression is forgiven, whose sin is covered.
Judge not, and ye shall not be judged: condemn not, and ye shall not be condemned: forgive, and ye shall be forgiven.
The LORD is merciful and gracious, slow to anger, and plenteous in mercy.
As far as the east is from the west, so far hath he removed our transgressions from us.
And forgive us our debts, as we forgive our debtors.
And Jesus said unto her, Neither do I condemn thee: go, and sin no more.
Doth not behave itself unseemly, seeketh not her own, is not easily provoked, thinketh no evil.
Forbearing one another, and forgiving one another, if any man have a quarrel against any.
The fear of the LORD is the beginning of wisdom: but fools despise wisdom and instruction.
If any of you lack wisdom, let him ask of God, that giveth to all men liberally, and upbraideth not; and it shall be given him.
Happy is the man that findeth wisdom, and the man that getteth understanding.
For wisdom is better than rubies; and all the things that may be desired are not to be compared to it.
Therefore whosoever heareth these sayings of mine, and doeth them, I will liken him unto a wise man, which built his house upon a rock.
But the wisdom that is from above is first pure, then peaceable, gentle, and easy to be intreated, full of mercy and good fruits.
Correct thy son, and he shall give thee rest; yea, he shall give delight unto thy soul.
The words of wise men are heard in quiet more than the cry of him that ruleth among fools.
The mouth of the just bringeth forth wisdom: but the froward tongue shall be cut out.
The LORD is nigh unto them that are of a broken heart; and saveth such as be of a contrite spirit.
And, lo, I am with you always, even unto the end of the world.
And God shall wipe away all tears from their eyes; and there shall be no more death, neither sorrow, nor crying, neither shall there be any more pain.
Yea, though I walk through the valley of the shadow of death, I will fear no evil: for thou art with me.
Cast thy burden upon the LORD, and he shall sustain thee: he shall never suffer the righteous to be moved.
God is our refuge and strength, a very present help in trouble.
The LORD openeth the eyes of the blind: the LORD raiseth them that are bowed down.
Then will I sprinkle clean water upon you, and ye shall be clean: from all your filthiness, and from all your idols, will I cleanse you.
